# Client OS Slice 1 Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Clients area (create client → onboarding upload → client-scoped research → foundation docs generated by a local Claude Code worker) with approve/reject learning loop.

**Architecture:** Deployed app (Render) is mission control — clients, jobs table as queue, statuses, document library. A worker script on the owner's Mac polls `/api/worker/claim`, runs headless `claude -p` with the owner's real agency skills + client context, posts four foundation docs back to `/api/worker/complete`. Rejection feedback is triaged into client lessons (DB) vs craft lessons (git-versioned `worker/learnings/*.md`).

**Tech Stack:** Existing: Vite/React 19, tRPC 11, Drizzle + TiDB (direct ALTER for schema), Express. New: `pdf-parse` for PDF text extraction, `scripts/worker.ts` run via tsx.

## Global Constraints

- TiDB rejects JSON DEFAULT; schema changes applied via direct `ALTER` script with DATABASE_URL pulled from Render API (no local .env).
- Deploys MUST pin explicit `commitId` and verify the live commit afterwards.
- No em dashes in user-visible UI copy. Clean, minimal UI ("less is more").
- Worker endpoints authenticated with `Bearer WORKER_SECRET`.
- Never mutate the claude.ai skills cache; learnings live in `worker/learnings/`.

---

### Task 1: Schema + DB helpers

**Files:**
- Modify: `drizzle/schema.ts` (clients, clientDocuments, jobs tables; miningSearches.clientId)
- Modify: `server/db.ts` (CRUD helpers)
- Create: scratch ALTER script (run once, not committed)

**Interfaces (produces):**
- Tables: `clients(id, userId, name, niche, funnelType, pricePoint, createdAt, updatedAt)`, `client_documents(id, clientId, kind, docType, title, content LONGTEXT, source, createdAt, updatedAt)`, `jobs(id, clientId, userId, type, status, payload JSON, error, createdAt, startedAt, finishedAt)`; `mining_searches.clientId INT NULL`.
- Helpers: `createClient`, `getClientsByUser`, `getClientById`, `updateClient`, `deleteClient`, `createClientDocument`, `getClientDocuments(clientId)`, `updateClientDocument`, `deleteClientDocument`, `createJob`, `getJobById`, `getJobsByClient`, `claimNextQueuedJob()` (atomic UPDATE ... status='running' LIMIT 1 then SELECT), `completeJob(id)`, `failJob(id, error)`, `setJobStatus`, `getSearchesByClient`.

Steps: write schema, write helpers, run ALTER against TiDB, `npm run check`, commit.

### Task 2: Clients tRPC router + PDF extraction

**Files:**
- Modify: `server/routers.ts` (new `clients` router)
- Modify: `package.json` (add `pdf-parse`)

**Interfaces (produces):**
- `clients.create({name, niche, funnelType, pricePoint?}) -> Client`
- `clients.list() -> Client[] with docCount/reportCount`
- `clients.get({id}) -> {client, documents (content omitted in list, fetched per doc), searches, reports, latestFoundationJob}`
- `clients.addTextDocument({clientId, docType, title, content})`
- `clients.addPdfDocument({clientId, docType, title, base64})` — extracts text via pdf-parse, stores text + source filename
- `clients.updateDocument({id, content})`, `clients.deleteDocument({id})`
- `clients.delete({id})`
- `mining.create` gains optional `clientId`

Steps: implement, typecheck, commit.

### Task 3: Foundation jobs — queue, review, worker HTTP endpoints

**Files:**
- Modify: `server/routers.ts` (`clients.generateFoundation`, `clients.reviewFoundation`)
- Create: `server/workerRoutes.ts` (express: `POST /api/worker/claim`, `POST /api/worker/complete`, `POST /api/worker/fail` with Bearer WORKER_SECRET)
- Modify: `server/_core/index.ts` (mount worker routes), `server/_core/env.ts` (workerSecret)
- Test: `server/worker.test.ts` (auth check unit test on the route handler)

**Interfaces (produces):**
- `generateFoundation({clientId, feedback?})` → creates job `{type:'foundation', status:'queued', payload:{feedback?, clientLessons: string[]}}`
- `reviewFoundation({clientId, action:'approve'|'reject', feedback?})` — approve: job→'approved'; reject: requeues new job with feedback in payload
- `POST /api/worker/claim` → `{job: {id, type, client:{...}, onboardingDocs:[{title,docType,content}], research: string, lessons: string[], feedback?} } | {job:null}`
- `POST /api/worker/complete` body `{jobId, docs:{icp_snapshot, offers, brand_positioning, course_outline}, clientLessons?: string[], craftLessons?: [{skill, lesson}]}` — upserts 4 foundation client_documents, saves lessons, job→'review'
- Research render: reuse report content serialized as readable text (keyword, pains, desires, quotes, competitor intel).

Steps: failing auth test → routes → pass → commit.

### Task 4: Local worker script + learnings

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/worker.ts` (poll loop, prompt assembly, headless claude run, output parsing, POST complete/fail)
- Create: `worker/learnings/README.md` (+ git-managed learnings dir)
- Modify: `package.json` (script `"worker": "tsx scripts/worker.ts"`)
- Test: `server/workerPrompt.test.ts` for `buildFoundationPrompt` + `parseFoundationOutputs` (pure functions exported from `scripts/workerLib.ts`)

**Interfaces:**
- Env: `APP_URL`, `WORKER_SECRET`, `SKILLS_DIR` (claude.ai agent cache skills path), `LEARNINGS_DIR` (default `worker/learnings`)
- `buildFoundationPrompt(job, skillsDir, learnings)` → prompt string instructing headless Claude to read the child skill files in mother-skill order and write `01_icp_snapshot.md`, `02_offers.md`, `03_brand_positioning.md`, `04_course_outline.md`, `client_lessons.md` (only when feedback present), `craft_lessons.md` into the job output dir.
- Runner: `claude -p <promptfile> --dangerously-skip-permissions` with cwd = job temp dir; 30-min timeout; parse output files; on missing docs → fail job with detail.
- Triage: when job payload has feedback, prompt includes triage instructions (client lesson vs craft lesson); worker appends craft lessons to `worker/learnings/<skill>.md` and git-commits with source stamp.

Steps: failing tests for the two pure functions → implement lib → pass → implement runner → commit.

### Task 5: Clients UI — list + create

**Files:**
- Create: `client/src/pages/Clients.tsx`
- Modify: `client/src/App.tsx` (routes `/clients`, `/clients/:id`), `client/src/components/AppShell.tsx` (nav item "Clients", top position)

Minimal: page header + "New Client" button opening inline form (name, niche, funnel type toggle webinar/call, price point optional) → rows: name, niche, funnel chip, stage summary. Click → detail.

Steps: build, typecheck, commit.

### Task 6: Client detail page — pipeline

**Files:**
- Create: `client/src/pages/ClientDetail.tsx`
- Modify: `client/src/pages/NewSearch.tsx` (accept `?client=ID`, pass clientId, show client badge)

Pipeline checklist (vertical stages with status chips):
1. Onboarding — PDF drag/drop (FileReader → base64 → addPdfDocument), paste form (docType select: voice transcript / competitors / intake / other), doc list with view/edit/delete.
2. Research — "Run Research" → `/search/new?client=ID`; list client reports linking to `/report/:id`.
3. Foundation — states: empty→"Generate Foundation Docs" (disabled until ≥1 onboarding doc, tooltip); queued/running→"Waiting for your Mac"/"Generating…" (poll every 5s); review→four doc cards (markdown shown as pre-wrap text, Edit toggles textarea, Save) + Approve + "Request changes" (textarea → reject);approved→green chip + docs + Regenerate.
4. Greyed future stages: Skool Community, Funnel, Emails, Ads — "coming next".

Steps: build, typecheck, lint pass on copy (no em dashes), commit.

### Task 7: Deploy + E2E

- Add `WORKER_SECRET` env on Render (generate), run ALTER script on TiDB, build+tests, commit, push, deploy pinned commit, verify live commit + health.
- Manual E2E with real Ibby PDF after user runs `npm run worker`.
- Tag `v2-client-os-slice1` after E2E confirmed.

## Self-review

- Spec coverage: engine (T3/T4), data model (T1), flow/UX (T5/T6), worker (T4), learning loop (T3 payload lessons + T4 triage + client lessons in claim payload), out-of-scope respected. ✓
- No placeholders beyond intentionally compact UI descriptions; exact interfaces defined. ✓
- Type consistency: docType strings match between T1/T2/T3/T4/T6. ✓
